title: VARA and DET Join Forces to Establish Best-in-class Market Assurance Standards for Virtual Assets
summary: On 10 August 2023, VARA -- which regulates virtual asset activity across Dubai's mainland and free zones, excluding the Dubai International Financial Centre (DIFC) -- and Dubai's Department of Economy and Tourism (DET) announced they had signed a Memorandum of Understanding establishing a joint framework for market oversight of the virtual assets sector across the Emirate of Dubai. Under the MoU, DET will incorporate VARA's licensing activities into its own system, conduct on-site inspections, and support VARA's enforcement actions, including suspensions or revocations for firms found non-compliant with VARA's rules. VARA will gain access to DET's E-Permit system for approvals related to virtual-asset events. The two bodies also agreed to coordinate consumer-protection campaigns, publish joint penalty notices and advisories, and share data under agreed data-sharing protocols.
why_it_matters: VARA is the authority that licenses and supervises virtual asset businesses operating in Dubai outside the DIFC free zone, and this MoU sets out how it will work with DET -- Dubai's licensing and consumer-protection authority -- on registration, inspections, and enforcement for that sector.

citations.quote: deliver effective and synchronised VA market assurance across the Emirate of Dubai
